title: Chapter 5 – Recognition
content:
"Crazy b*st*rd!"

"Kill him!"

They were the dead man's companions.

Their faces twisted with fury as they charged, but the boy did not move. His sword remained planted in the corpse.

His eyes were fixed on the Blade Sect Leader alone.

'Whether I die now or later.'

This was the day he had learned the devastating truth of his shortened lifespan.

One of the pillars holding his sanity together had crumbled entirely. It did not feel like something that would ever be rebuilt.

"Stop."

A deep voice, as though rising from the floor of a cavern.

The Blade Sect Leader's voice suited his overwhelming presence. The charging warriors froze mid-stride.

"Your spirit."

The Blade Sect Leader stared at the boy.

Those abyssal eyes bore into him, yet Jeong Yeon-shin met the gaze with his chest thrust forward, as if daring the man to gut him where he stood.

"It surpasses everyone in this estate combined."

The Blade Sect's warriors visibly faltered.

It was immediately clear that praise from this man was exceedingly rare.

"Just kill him and be done with it! Even if he's tied to the Desolate Sect, they can't question a bloodied corpse!"

Murder and destruction -- that was all this one spoke of.

He appeared to be a companion of the red-haired woman who had died last.

The same bright red hair, roughly shorn, and the same crimson martial uniform.

A warrior of the Blood Flame Sect who had accompanied the Blade Sect Leader.

'I hope they don't realize there's no real connection.'

In this moment, Jeong Yeon-shin felt the fearsome reach of the Thirteen Heavens with painful clarity.

The surprise attack had accounted for every factor. This was how the great sects that stretched their influence across the world operated.

They had known that the son of a place as insignificant as the Jeong estate held a connection to the Desolate Sect.

"Steward!"

Jeong Yeon-shin's voice rang out. The man sprawled in the distant doorway flinched.

He had been playing dead.

"Y-yes, young master."

The steward staggered to his feet as he answered -- no ordinary man himself. Jeong Yeon-shin pressed on.

"You told me you were building an intelligence network across Xinye. These arrogant fools tried to slaughter the estate with barely a dozen men, without even establishing a perimeter. Surely the ears and mouths of the Jeong estate reach at least as far as Xiangyang?"

"No, Xiangyang... Xiangyang is within reach."

Xiangyang, in the northern reaches of Huguang Province, was a border city of the Nanyang prefecture to which Xinye County belonged.

Vast as the Central Plains were, this much was close.

Close enough that an intelligence agent with martial training could reach it at a sprint without serious incident.

'In truth, I'm not even sure they actually sent for help from the Desolate Sect.'

It was unclear whether the family had ever designated Jeong Yeon-shin's maternal clan as their recourse in times of crisis.

But even the Blade Sect Leader could not peer into the internal affairs of a minor family like the Jeongs beyond what blood ties revealed.

"If you can't kill me."

Jeong Yeon-shin tightened his grip on the sword.

"Don't make another move."

He drew the blade from the dead man's back. The long trail of blood settled over the estate like silence itself.

A boy who looked no older than fifteen had seized control of a place occupied by the leader and warriors of a major sect.

The Blood Flame Sect member shouted in rage.

"We've already earned your grudge! How can we leave you alive when you'll obviously run your mouth!"

Though his words were aimed at Jeong Yeon-shin, they were really a protest to the Blade Sect Leader.

This moment was critical. The atmosphere had tilted, as though the roles of predator and prey had suddenly reversed.

Jeong Yeon-shin's reply dripped with contempt.

"Fool. Even if the Desolate Sect stands above all others in the martial world, would they directly attack the Thirteen Heavens? Over the grandson of a former leader? Does that make any sense to you?"

If he were the grandson of the Desolate Sect Lord, perhaps.

The Desolate Sect Lord, recognized by the emperor himself as equal to a prince, was the guardian of the great World Tree and keeper of the fruit said to grant immortality to those who consumed it.

But Jeong Yeon-shin did not carry the Desolate Sect Lord's bloodline.

Had he, the members of the Jeong estate would never have been swayed by superstitious unease, regardless of the circumstances of his birth.

"The grandson of Desolate Sect's former Sect Leader?"

Precipice Sword of the Zhongnan Sect, who had been tending to Yeo Il-shin's wounds, murmured.

A boy whose maternal grandfather was the retired leader of the Desolate Sect's martial organization.

His status made the consequences of killing him far harder to predict than the trouble of letting him live.

The Thirteen Heavens were great names that claimed hegemony across the world.

Yet harming the grandson of a Desolate Sect elder was another matter entirely.

It carried a weight comparable to taking the family of a Hanlin Academy scholar in the imperial capital hostage.

"The imperial sect, established by noble clans to prevent insurrection in the martial world. Certainly troublesome."

The Blade Sect Leader spoke.

"But that is not my reason for letting you live."

"Sect Leader!"

Splat--!

The shouting Blood Flame Sect member's head burst apart.

He had not even sensed the attack before an invisible blade of sword energy erupted.

"Blood Flame Sect. I told you to stop."

The Blade Sect Leader addressed the corpse.

The remaining red-haired warrior trembled, but the leader's dark eyes had already returned to Jeong Yeon-shin.

"I like you. That talent and that spirit. You have the makings to reach the pinnacle of the martial world. If you were not tied to the Desolate Sect, I would have taken you as my disciple."

"Gasp!"

"The Blade Sect Leader, he..."

Shocked murmurs broke out on all sides.

Not only his subordinates, but even the Zhongnan Sect experts could not conceal their astonishment.

The Blade Sect Leader continued, indifferent to their reactions.

"I will accept a duel anytime. Come and take your revenge."

"......"

Jeong Yeon-shin sheathed his sword. He turned his back and walked toward the estate members who still drew faint breaths.

The Blade Sect Leader watched the boy go with an unreadable expression, then turned to the Zhongnan Sect warriors.

"This land. I no longer permit other sects to set foot here. You have one day."

His gaze settled on Yeo Il-shin, whose face had gone rigid.

"Leave."

* * *

"Young master."

"Steward."

There was no time for a proper reunion.

The corpses would have to wait.

A doctor was present, but no healer of miraculous skill like Hua Tuo -- for those in critical condition, the most that could be done was to let them rest in comfort.

He found his eldest brother Jeong Nam-san and his sister-in-law. They were already dead.

Carefully moving their bodies aside, he stomped hard on the floor where they had fallen.

Clunk.

The ground opened to reveal a hidden chamber -- the secret room where the brothers used to huddle together and giggle as small children.

Sure enough, a five-year-old girl lay curled up inside, eyes shut.

Her acupoints had been sealed. Someone had pressed the sleep point at the base of her neck with qi-infused fingers, forcing her into unconsciousness.

"Hye-ah."

Jeong Yeon-shin gathered his niece into his arms and carried her out.

A young man who had been speaking with the steward turned his head.

It was Jeong Jung-san, the second son of the Jeong family, slated to become a named disciple of the Zhongnan Sect.

The way he averted his gaze the instant their eyes met told Jeong Yeon-shin everything. He had been hiding somewhere.

Jeong Yeon-shin approached in silence and thrust Hye-ah into his arms.

"Raise her in Zhongnan. By whatever means necessary."

"You -- what about you?"

"I'll be busy looking after myself."

Jeong Yeon-shin's tone left no room for argument.

"Raise her in Zhongnan? How could that possibly..."

Unlike before, Jeong Jung-san stammered without even meeting his eyes.

It seemed he had hidden near the main building and watched his half-brother fight the entire time.

Pathetic fool. Jeong Yeon-shin finally registered the strange sensation on his cheek and wiped the dried blood away with the back of his hand.

"You know the Nine Sects better than I do. Children of five or six become disciples and eventually the backbone of the sect. Find a way. Beg if you must. They'll take her -- Hye-ah's aptitude is better than yours."

"R-right."

Jeong Jung-san bit his lip and looked down at his niece.

Jeong Yeon-shin studied him for a long moment. Fortunately, what he saw in those eyes was responsibility rather than contempt.

Just then, the Zhongnan Sect experts approached.

As always, Yeo Il-shin led, with Precipice Sword and a warrior known as Shadowless Fist following half a step behind.

Despite their pallid complexions and the bloodstains from internal injuries, these people had stayed to help with the aftermath. They were righteous martial artists, through and through.

"Young hero."

The form of address had changed. Only yesterday, they had called him nothing more than the family head's youngest son.

Yeo Il-shin's expression was layered with complexity.

Beyond self-reproach, her eyes held genuine amazement as she regarded Jeong Yeon-shin -- the look of someone confronting a pillar of talent beyond anything she had imagined.

"Are you going to the Desolate Sect?"

"I suppose I must. There's nowhere else I can rely on to grow stronger."

"The road doesn't only lead to Xiangyang."

"...?"

"Come climb Mount Zhongnan with us. I recommend it sincerely."

"Huh!"

Only the steward and Jeong Jung-san were stunned.

Precipice Sword and Shadowless Fist stood behind her with unchanged expressions, as though the invitation were the most natural thing in the world.

Their faces were unmistakable -- the hunger for talent.

It meant he was an extraordinary young swordsman, one worthy of a direct invitation from a sect's great elder.

"For a great elder of the Nine Sects to... I truly don't know how to respond."

Despite his words, Jeong Yeon-shin's expression remained calm. He had already chosen his destination.

Yeo Il-shin seemed to sense his hesitation and pressed further.

"I intend to recommend you as the sect leader's personal disciple. With your abilities, it is entirely possible."

"...!"

This time, no one could contain their shock.

The position of personal disciple to the Zhongnan Sect leader meant learning peerless divine arts.

It also meant gaining the vast backing of one of the Nine Schools.

Moreover, the current Zhongnan sect leader was invariably named in any discussion of the Ten Great Swordsmen of the world.

His formidable swordsmanship, which he delighted in displaying through chivalrous deeds, had been witnessed by the public on countless occasions.

'The people of Shaanxi hail him as one of the Five Great Masters of the Central Plains!'

The steward screamed inwardly.

He must go. No matter how powerful the Desolate Sect was, Jeong Yeon-shin was merely the grandson of a retired warrior there.

The ceiling might be uncertain, but the floor as the Zhongnan sect leader's disciple was surely higher.

It was the typical thinking of the Jeong estate -- always preferring the head of a snake to the tail of a dragon.

'Young master! Please!'

His silent plea did not reach Jeong Yeon-shin.

The boy clasped his fist in a formal salute.

"I will carry your offer in my heart, senior."

A polite refusal.

Resignation passed across Yeo Il-shin's elegant features. The speed with which she accepted it suggested she had expected nothing less.

"You must be confident, young hero. Confident that you can rise higher within the Desolate Sect."

"......"

"I coveted that vessel of yours. The Blade Sect Leader must have felt the same."

"I'm sorry."

"There's no need. Just know that Zhongnan's doors will always be open to you."

Jeong Yeon-shin held her gaze for a moment before speaking.

"I understand you are of advanced age among the noble clans, Great Elder. Perhaps you know of a way to obtain the World Tree's fruit?"

"The World Tree's fruit! Then you truly are...!"

Realization dawned on Yeo Il-shin's face, but it quickly gave way to regret.

"I have not lived even half my allotted lifespan. I hold no authority over the World Tree. Only the Desolate Sect Leader can grant its fruit to others."

"Then the Desolate Sect it is."

"It will not be easy. That is a sacred object even among us noble clans."

"I have no other choice -- I know of no other way. I'll have to try becoming the Sect Leader, or achieve some feat great enough to merit it."

"I'll support you from afar."

Yeo Il-shin returned his salute. Her righteous character was something Jeong Yeon-shin suspected he would remember for a very long time.

"Well then."

The courtesies they exchanged served as farewell.

Mount Zhongnan in Xi'an, Shaanxi, and Xiangyang lay in entirely different directions, and she wished to depart quickly to report to her sect -- alone, if need be.

The qinggong of Precipice Sword and Shadowless Fist fell short of the great elder's, and they would only slow her down.

Jeong Jung-san decided to leave the following day with the two remaining Zhongnan warriors.

But for now, there was a deathbed to attend.

The family head was still alive. Barely.

"It's me. Your third son."

He knelt on one knee and cradled the back of his father's neck. Jeong Yeon-shin's eyes trembled faintly.

The sword wound carved into the heartless family head's abdomen was beyond any hope of recovery.

If you were going to die like this, you should have treated me better.

"Why are you... why are you so strong? How did you..."

His lips quivered, as though every last scrap of strength had drained from his body.

Jeong Yeon-shin knew time was short. It was something close to premonition. Jeong Daemyeong, the family head, would manage only a few more words before the end.

"What I created and mastered on my own -- I call it the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique."

Rather than voice his resentment and grief, he chose to boast of his own achievement.

How the family head took it was his to decide.

Jeong Yeon-shin, long since weary of family, left it to his father's heart to determine whether this was a gift or a final act of filial defiance.

"Jeong Family Dynamic Technique. I see."

The family head parted his trembling lips.

"A divine art... created by a Jeong. This foolish, ignorant... Jeong Dae-myeong. The son I sired -- the third son of an incompetent family head -- created a divine art."

"......"

The boy listened in silence. His father spoke for the last time.

"You have... the qualities... of a great master..."
